text: With "required conditions" I meant the code in your platform, that ensures that the diagrams do what they are supposed to do -- but then use an LLM to generate the diagrams (in whatever format it is that you have diagrams). Does this make a bit more sense?

elements.text: With "required conditions" I meant the code in your platform, that ensures that the diagrams do what they are supposed to do -- but then use an LLM to generate the diagrams (in whatever format it is that you have diagrams). Does this make a bit more sense?

text: Unfortunately I'm still not following. A users needs to specify somehow what the diagram should contain. In Penrose, the user writes some domain specific code. That code is declarative and tells the system what they need/want of the diagram. Perhaps that is what you mean by "required conditions." We then compile the domain-specific code to a numeric optimization problem. Penrose then optimizes the energy function.  Given all that background, what are you suggesting the LLM should do? Generate the specification? Generate the diagram when given a specification? Solve the optimization problem? Something else?

text: Ah, sorry, I misunderstood. With the background, I would say, a) generate the specification.
